methods.method_name: Watermelon Seedling Transplantation and Plot Setup
methods.description: Process for transplanting watermelon seedlings into experimental plots and setting up the experimental design.

steps.description: On 3 Apr. and 1 Aug. 2006, 4-week-old watermelon seedlings were transplanted into raised beds covered with black polyethylene mulch in the spring and white on black polyethylene mulch in the fall.
steps.searchable_summary: transplant watermelon seedlings into raised beds with polyethylene mulch

steps.description: The experimental design was a randomized complete block with four replications.
steps.searchable_summary: set up randomized complete block design with four replications

steps.description: Three pollenizer seedlings were planted at even spacing in the center of each plot.
steps.searchable_summary: plant pollenizer seedlings at even spacing in plots

methods.description: Procedure for collecting watermelon pollen samples and testing their viability using the diaminobenzidine (DAB) test.

steps.description: In the spring, sampling was initiated on 17 May with additional pollen samples taken on 24 May and 31 May. For the fall, sampling was initiated on 31 Aug. with other pollen samples taken on 7 Sept. and 14 Sept.
steps.searchable_summary: schedule and collect pollen samples on specified dates

steps.description: On the sampling dates, watermelon flowers were removed from the plant before the flowers opened.
steps.searchable_summary: remove unopened watermelon flowers for pollen collection

steps.description: Flowers were taken to the laboratory and allowed to open. Samples were analyzed after anther dehiscence was verified using a hand lens.
steps.searchable_summary: allow flowers to open and verify anther dehiscence
outputs: opened flowers with verified anther dehiscence

steps.description: Pollen was removed from the anthers and placed on a slide. Viability was determined using the diaminobenzidine (DAB) test for peroxidase activity in pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DAB test for pollen viability

steps.description: On each sample date, heat-killed pollen (2 h at 80°C) was analyzed to prevent false-positive readings.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze heat-killed pollen to prevent false positives

steps.description: Data were taken as percent viable pollen and square root transformations were performed. Analysis of variance (PROC GLM) and means separation (Duncan's multiple range test) were accomplished using SAS version 9.1.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen viability data using ANOVA and Duncan's test
